findings.signature: INSERT INTO memory_idempotency_receipts ... ON CONFLICT(receipt_key) DO UPDATE SET
findings.notes: Direct INSERT/UPSERT with datetime('now') — Turso supports ON CONFLICT but datetime() function behavior may differ; write path must verify identical semantics.

findings.signature: SELECT payload_hash, response_payload FROM memory_idempotency_receipts WHERE receipt_key = ?
findings.notes: Synchronous better-sqlite3 .prepare().get() — Turso async API requires wrapper; read-only but part of write-critical idempotency flow.

findings.signature: UPDATE memory_index SET near_duplicate_of = NULL, last_dedup_checked_at = NULL WHERE id = ?
findings.notes: Direct UPDATE on memory_index — Turso supports standard UPDATE; synchronous .run() call needs async adaptation.

findings.signature: UPDATE memory_index SET near_duplicate_of = ?, last_dedup_checked_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?
findings.notes: UPDATE with datetime('now') and JSON.stringify payload — write path in dedup flow; datetime() and JSON.stringify must behave identically in Turso.

findings.signature: SELECT updated_at, last_dedup_checked_at FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?
findings.notes: Read gate for write path; synchronous better-sqlite3 .prepare().get() needs async wrapper for Turso.
Pure computation producing insert/upsert/replace/delete action descriptors — no direct SQLite coupling; action types are DB-agnostic. Migration-neutral write planner.
Write dispatcher via StatediffTargetSink.apply() — already async-ready; Turso migration moves actual SQL into sink implementations, not this module.

findings.signature: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'
findings.notes: FTS5 existence check via sqlite_master — Turso lacks FTS5; this check would always return false, disabling the entire FTS channel.
Delegates to fts5Bm25Search which uses bm25(memory_fts, ...) — FTS5 bm25() function unavailable in Turso; entire FTS search channel must be replaced.

findings.signature: SELECT m.* FROM memory_index m JOIN active_memory_projection p ... WHERE LOWER(m.trigger_phrases) LIKE ?
findings.notes: Standard SQL with LIKE and JOIN — Turso-compatible; uses synchronous better-sqlite3 .prepare().all() needing async wrapper.

findings.signature: SELECT id, title, file_path, importance_tier, importance_weight, spec_folder FROM memory_index WHERE ... ORDER BY CASE importance_tier ...
findings.notes: Pure standard SQL fallback — Turso-compatible CASE/ORDER BY; no FTS5 or extensions needed.
Converts embeddings to Buffer for sqlite-vec storage format — Turso/vector-index replacement must handle identical binary serialization or provide compatible adapter.

findings.signature: db.prepare('SELECT id, spec_folder, importance_tier FROM memory_index WHERE id IN (...)')
findings.notes: BM25 scope resolution via standard SQL — Turso-compatible; but the BM25 lexical index (getIndex()) is in-memory, not SQLite-coupled.
Pure computation over result arrays — no direct SQLite/driver coupling; migration-neutral.
Pure string transformation pipeline — no direct SQLite/driver coupling; migration-neutral.
Pure text chunking logic — no direct SQLite/driver coupling; migration-neutral.
Tests DB handle liveness via synchronous better-sqlite3 prepare — Turso async API needs different probe; SELECT 1 is standard SQL.
Filesystem-only path resolution for DB directory — no direct SQLite/driver coupling; migration-neutral.
